claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 14 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 14 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Blake
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 14 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's get started. To determine your eligibility for Child Benefit, I first need to know about your child.

**Question 1: How old is your child?**

This is important because:
- Children **under 16** generally qualify automatically (subject to other conditions)
- Children **aged 16-19** may qualify if they're in approved education or training
- Children **aged 20 or over** do not qualify

Please tell me your child's age, and if you have multiple children, we can assess each one.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Thanks for helping me with this. I have two children I'm asking about - Alex is 8 years old and Blake is 14 years old. They both live at home with me here in the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! That's very helpful. You have:
- **Alex** (8 years old)
- **Blake** (14 years old)

Both are under 16, so they're in the age range that generally qualifies. Let me continue with the assessment for children under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Question 2: Are Alex and Blake working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

Given their ages (8 and 14), they're likely in school and not in full-time work, but I need to confirm this to proceed. 

Are either of them working 24 hours or more per week in paid employment?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them work at all - they're both still in school full-time.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's what I expected for children of their ages. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Question 3: Are Alex and Blake receiving any benefits in their own right?**

For example, are either of them receiving:
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit
- Or similar benefits in their own name?

(This would be unusual for children aged 8 and 14, but I need to check to ensure they qualify.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them receive any benefits in their own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Question 4: Are Alex or Blake in any type of apprenticeship?**

Given their ages (8 and 14), this is unlikely, but I need to confirm:
- Are either of them enrolled in any apprenticeship programs?

(Different apprenticeship types in England, Scotland, and Wales have different rules, but at their ages, they would typically be in standard education.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them are in any apprenticeships - they're just in regular school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Now let me move on to questions about you as the claimant.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Question 5: Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You mentioned you live in the UK with your children. To confirm eligibility, I need to check if you meet the residency requirements. You can qualify through ANY of these paths:

1. **You normally live in the UK** (this is the most common route)
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have pre-settled status and meet certain conditions (working, studying, or have sufficient resources)
4. You're a Crown servant living abroad

Based on what you've told me, do you normally live in the UK? Or do any of the other circumstances apply to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I normally live in the UK - we all live here together.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! That satisfies the residency requirement. Now let me check about your responsibility for the children.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Question 6: Are you responsible for Alex and Blake?**

You've already mentioned they both live at home with you, which clearly establishes responsibility. Just to confirm:

**Do Alex and Blake live with you?** 

(You can also be considered responsible if you contribute to their upkeep even if they don't live with you, but it sounds like you all live together.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - they both live at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Question 7: Is anyone else claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Blake?**

For example:
- Is another parent or guardian currently claiming Child Benefit for either child?
- Has anyone else made a claim for them?

Only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child at a time. If there's another claimant and you both agree on who should claim, that's fine. But I need to know the situation.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! Let me move to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Question 8: Are Alex and Blake living normally at home with you, or are there any special circumstances?**

I need to check if either child is:
- Currently in hospital for an extended period
- In local authority care
- Being fostered
- Or living normally at home with you

What's the situation for Alex and Blake?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They're both just living normally at home with me - no special circumstances like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Question 9: Is the local council paying towards Alex or Blake's accommodation or maintenance?**

In other words:
- Is the local council contributing financially to either child's living costs or accommodation?

(This would be unusual since they live with you, but I need to confirm this final point.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything towards them - I'm supporting them myself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me get the final outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## 🎉 Great News! You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit!

You meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for both **Alex** and **Blake**.

### **Why You're Eligible:**

✅ **Alex (8 years old)** qualifies because:
- Under 16 years old
- In full-time education, not working
- Not receiving benefits in their own right
- Living with you at home

✅ **Blake (14 years old)** qualifies because:
- Under 16 years old  
- In full-time education, not working
- Not receiving benefits in their own right
- Living with you at home

✅ **You as the claimant** meet all requirements:
- Normally resident in the UK
- Responsible for both children (they live with you)
- No one else is claiming for them
- The local council isn't paying for their care

---

### **Next Steps to Claim:**

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and both children
3. **Provide birth certificates** or adoption certificates for Alex and Blake

---

### **Important Information:**

⚠️ **High Income Child Benefit Charge**: If you or your partner earns over £60,000 per year, you may need to pay a tax charge. You can still claim Child Benefit but may choose not to receive payments while still getting National Insurance credits.

💷 **Payment schedule**: Child Benefit is usually paid every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits).

📋 **More information and to apply**: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim

---

Is there anything else you'd like to know about claiming Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex (8 years old) meets all eligibility requirements: (1) Under 16 years old - qualifies automatically for age requirement, (2) Not working 24+ hours per week - confirmed in full-time school, (3) Not receiving benefits in own right, (4) Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship - in regular school, (5) Lives with claimant who is responsible for them, (6) No other person claiming Child Benefit for them, (7) Living normally at home, (8) Local council not paying towards accommodation or maintenance.

Blake (14 years old) meets all eligibility requirements: (1) Under 16 years old - qualifies automatically for age requirement, (2) Not working 24+ hours per week - confirmed in full-time school, (3) Not receiving benefits in own right, (4) Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship - in regular school, (5) Lives with claimant who is responsible for them, (6) No other person claiming Child Benefit for them, (7) Living normally at home, (8) Local council not paying towards accommodation or maintenance.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for both children, Alex (8) and Blake (14). Both children meet all the qualifying child criteria as they are under 16, in full-time education, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in disqualifying apprenticeships. The claimant meets all the claimant requirements: normally resident in the UK, responsible for both children (living with them), no other person claiming for these children, and the local council is not paying towards the children's accommodation or maintenance.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements
next_steps: Apply online or by post using form CH2
Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex (8 years old) meets all eligibility requirements: (1) Under 16 years old - qualifies automatically for age requirement, (2) Not working 24+ hours per week - confirmed in full-time school, (3) Not receiving benefits in own right, (4) Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship - in regular school, (5) Lives with claimant who is responsible for them, (6) No other person claiming Child Benefit for them, (7) Living normally at home, (8) Local council not paying towards accommodation or maintenance.

Blake (14 years old) meets all eligibility requirements: (1) Under 16 years old - qualifies automatically for age requirement, (2) Not working 24+ hours per week - confirmed in full-time school, (3) Not receiving benefits in own right, (4) Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship - in regular school, (5) Lives with claimant who is responsible for them, (6) No other person claiming Child Benefit for them, (7) Living normally at home, (8) Local council not paying towards accommodation or maintenance.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for both children, Alex (8) and Blake (14). Both children meet all the qualifying child criteria as they are under 16, in full-time education, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in disqualifying apprenticeships. The claimant meets all the claimant requirements: normally resident in the UK, responsible for both children (living with them), no other person claiming for these children, and the local council is not paying towards the children's accommodation or maintenance.
